title: Age equality in UK healthcare policy and decision-making: A scoping review
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3fa9d","text":"The review investigates whether—and how—age influences the provision of healthcare in the United Kingdom, including the justifications (or lack thereof) for age-based decisions, and the resulting impacts across the life course. The aim is to understand whether healthcare policies and decisions treat individuals differently based on age, and what normative reasoning underpins such differences. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cm1oc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qfqn","text":"We searched nine databases on 24th October 2024, with an update on 26th September 2025: Medline(OvidSP)[1946-], HMIC(OvidSP)[1979 to July 2024], EconLit[EBSCOHost], PAIS International, Policy File, ASSIA, Sociological Abstracts \u00026 Sociology Database[Proquest] and Overton https://www.overton.io/ . We searched Google on 25th October 2024, screening the 1st 100 results for each search string, if the 1st 50 references weren't relevant we stopped. The search strategy included title, abstract, author keywords and subject headings for our key concepts of age-related decision making, health policy and ethics. We applied a geographical search filter developed by information specialists at NICE. Non-English studies and references published before 2009 were excluded. The results were exported to Endnote for deduplication and storage and transferred to Covidence for screening. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvp57","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a336","text":"Manual searches were also conducted to capture grey literature outside academic databases, this particularly focused on decision-making guidance and frameworks in NHS units.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cil83","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Record the following information for each database and platform searched: database name, platform used, coverage period, date of search, and number of hits retrieved.
Remove duplicate records from the combined search results.
Exclude studies that are non-UK or published before 2009. Document the number of excluded studies for each criterion (e.g., Non-UK studies: 88, Pre2009: 207).
Calculate and record the final total number of included studies after deduplication and exclusions (e.g., Final total: 619).
Export the deduplicated search results to Endnote for storage, then transfer the records to Covidence for screening.
Construct and execute the Medline (Ovid MEDLINE) search using the following steps:
Combine the following search terms and subject headings to capture age-related concepts, health policy, ethics, and UK-specific studies. Use the provided line numbers to structure the search logic:
1. Search for age-related concepts using subject headings and keywords (lines 1-13).
2. Search for health policy and resource allocation concepts using subject headings and keywords (lines 15-25).
3. Search for ethics, equity, and fairness concepts using subject headings and keywords (lines 26-30).
4. Combine the results from the age-related, health policy, and ethics searches (line 31: 14 and 25 and 30).
5. Apply UK-specific filters using subject headings and keywords (lines 32-39).
6. Combine UK-specific filters (line 40: 32 or 33 or 34 or 35 or 36 or 37 or 38 or 39).

7. Exclude non-UK regions by subtracting global regions (line 41: (exp africa/ or exp americas/ or exp antarctic regions/ or exp arctic regions/ or exp asia/ or exp oceania/) not (exp great britain/ or europe/)).
8. Apply the final UK filter (line 42: 40 not 41).
9. Combine the main search (step 4) with the UK filter (step 8) to produce the final set of results (line 43: 31 and 42).
Construct and execute the HMIC (Health Management Information Consortium) search using the following steps:
1. Search for age-related concepts using subject headings and keywords (lines 1-12).
2. Search for health policy and resource allocation concepts using subject headings and keywords (lines 14-22).
3. Search for ethics, equity, and fairness concepts using subject headings and keywords (lines 24-28).
4. Combine the results from the age-related, health policy, and ethics searches (line 30: 13 and 23 and 29).
Construct and execute the Scopus search using the following steps:

1. Search for age-related concepts using the following logic (S1): ((TI,AB,IF(age) NEAR/3 (TI,AB,IF(discriminat*) OR TI,AB,IF(bias) OR TI,AB,IF(prejudice))) OR (TI,AB,IF(ageist) OR TI,AB,IF(ageism)) OR ((TI,AB,IF(age) OR TI,AB,IF(aged) OR TI,AB,IF(ageing) OR TI,AB,IF(aging) OR TI,AB,IF(young) OR TI,AB,IF(youth) OR TI,AB,IF(child*) OR TI,AB,IF(p?ediatric*)) NEAR/3 (TI,AB,IF(barrier?) OR TI,AB,IF(limit?) OR TI,AB,IF("cut off*"))) OR (TI,AB,IF(age) NEAR/5 (TI,AB,IF(assumption?) OR TI,AB,IF(presumption?) OR TI,AB,IF(supposition?) OR TI,AB,IF(belief?) OR TI,AB,IF(attitude*))) OR (TI,AB,IF(life) PRE/0 (TI,AB,IF(change) OR TI,AB,IF(course) OR TI,AB,IF(span) OR TI,AB,IF(stage?))) OR ((TI(transfer*) OR TI(transition*)) AND (TI(p?ediatric*) OR TI(child*) OR TI(adolescen*) OR TI(teen*) OR TI(youth) OR TI(young)) AND TI(adult) AND (TI(care) OR TI(health*) OR TI(service?))) OR (TI,AB,IF(menopaus*) OR TI,AB,IF(premenopaus*) OR TI,AB,IF(perimenopaus*) OR TI,AB,IF(postmenopaus*))).

2. Search for health policy and resource allocation concepts using the following logic (S2): (TI,AB,IF(health*) PRE/0 (TI,AB,IF(system?) OR TI,AB,IF(policy) OR TI,AB,IF(policies))) OR ((TI(health*) OR TI(care) OR TI(service?)) AND (TI(system?) OR TI(policy) OR TI(policies) OR TI(reform?) OR TI(allocat*) OR TI(ration*) OR TI(reorgani*) OR TI(re-organi*) OR TI(priorit?ation) OR TI("priority setting") OR TI(priorities) OR TI(prioriti?ing) OR TI(access*) OR TI("decision making") OR TI(judg?ment?))) OR ((AB(health*) OR AB(care) OR AB(service?)) NEAR/5 (AB(system?) OR AB(policy) OR AB(policies) OR AB(reform?) OR AB(allocat*) OR AB(ration*) OR AB(reorgani*) OR AB(re-organi*) OR AB(priorit?ation) OR AB("priority setting") OR AB(priorities) OR AB(prioriti?ing) OR AB(access*) OR AB("decision making") OR AB(judg?ment?))) OR (TI,AB,IF(ration*) OR TI,AB,IF("priority setting") OR TI,AB,IF(priorities) OR TI,AB,IF(priorit?ation) OR TI,AB,IF(prioriti?ing))) OR (NOT(scarce) OR NOFT(scarcity)).

3. Search for ethics, equity, and fairness concepts using the following logic (S3): (TI(ethic*) OR TI(bioethic*)) OR AB(bioethic*) OR (TI,AB,IF(fair*) OR TI,AB,IF(equity) OR TI,AB,IF(equitable) OR TI,AB,IF(equalit*) OR TI,AB,IF(inequit*) OR TI,AB,IF(inequitable) OR TI,AB,IF(inequalit*)).
4. Combine the results from the age-related, health policy, and ethics searches (S4): [S1] AND [S2] AND [S3].
Record the number of results retrieved for each Scopus search set: S1, S2, S3, and S4. For example, S1: 2492, S2: 34788, S3: 109216, S4: 34.

Flow diagram showing the number of results for each Scopus search set: S1 (2492), S2 (34788), S3 (109216), S4 (34). Visualize the number of results for each Scopus search set (S1, S2, S3, S4) using a flow diagram or chart.
Combine additional Scopus search sets as needed. For example, S8: [S5] AND [S6] AND [S7], and record the number of results (e.g., S8: 149).
Combine further Scopus search sets as needed: for example, S9, S10, S11, S12, S13, S14, S15, and S16. Document the logic and results for each set.
Combine Scopus search sets S9, S10, and S11 using the logic: [S9] AND [S10] AND [S11].
Apply a location filter to the combined Scopus search sets: [S9] AND [S10] AND [S11] AND location.exact("United Kingdom--UK" OR "England" OR "Europe" OR "Ireland" OR "Scotland" OR "Wales").

Exclude records from the combined Scopus search sets that match non-target locations: [S9] AND [S10] AND [S11] NOT location.exact("United States--US" OR "Canada" OR "Australia" OR "China" OR "New York" OR "India" OR "Mexico" OR "New Zealand" OR "Uganda" OR "Denmark" OR "Latin America" OR "Venezuela" OR "Africa" OR "California" OR "Florida" OR "Japan" OR "Michigan" OR "Texas" OR "Appalachian Mountains" OR "Arizona" OR "Asia" OR "Baltimore Maryland" OR "Bangladesh" OR "Beijing China" OR "British Columbia Canada" OR "Canberra Australian Capital Territory Australia" OR "Chicago Illinois" OR "Colorado" OR "Delhi India" OR "Detroit Michigan" OR "Dhaka Bangladesh" OR "East Asia" OR "Finland" OR "France" OR "Ghana" OR "Idaho" OR "Illinois" OR "Iran" OR "Italy" OR "Karnataka India" OR "Kuwait" OR "Lithuania" OR "Long Island New York" OR "Malawi" OR "Malta" OR "Manitoba Canada" OR "Middle East" OR "Mississippi" OR "New Jersey" OR "New Mexico" OR "New South Wales Australia" OR "Nicaragua" OR "North Carolina" OR "Northern California" OR "Norway" OR "Poland" OR "Portugal" OR "Qatar" OR "San Francisco California" OR "Saskatchewan Canada" OR "South Africa" OR "South Asia" OR "South Korea" OR "Spain" OR "Sub-Saharan Africa" OR "Turkey" OR "Union of Soviet Socialist Republics--USSR" OR "Vancouver Island" OR "Victoria Australia" OR "Vietnam").
Subtract results from set S14 from S12: [S12] NOT [S14].
Combine results from sets S13 and S15 using OR logic: [S13] OR [S15].
Record the number of results for the following Scopus search sets: S12 NOT S14 (e.g., 384 results) and S13 OR S15 (e.g., 23 results).
Record the number of results for the following Scopus search sets: 273, 111, and 129.

For the UK-limited search terms, record the number of selected results for each search string as follows: title: age AND health* (23), abstract:age AND discrimination AND health* (6), abstract:age AND bias AND health* (0), abstract:ageism AND health* (0), abstract:age AND health* AND equity (1), abstract:age AND health* AND equality (2), abstract:age AND health* AND inequity (0), abstract:age AND health* AND inequality (3), abstract:age AND health* AND equitable (0), abstract:age AND health* AND inequitable (0), abstract:age AND health* AND ethics (0), abstract:age AND health* AND ethical (0), abstract:age AND health* AND bioethics (0), abstract:age AND health* AND bioethical (0), abstract:age AND health* AND fair (0), abstract:age AND health* AND fairness (0), abstract:age AND health* AND value* (1), abstract:age AND health* AND policy (4), abstract:age AND health* AND reform* (2), abstract:age AND health* AND ration* (0), abstract:age AND health* AND priority (0), abstract:age AND health* AND priorities (2), abstract:age AND health* AND prioritisation (0), abstract:age AND health* AND scarcity (0). Record the total number of selected results (44).

For web-based UK-limited search terms, record the number of selected results for each search string and site as follows: - age AND (barriers OR limits OR bias OR discrimination) AND (health OR healthcare) site:nhs.uk, 1st 100 screened: 7 selected - age AND (barriers OR limits OR bias OR discrimination) AND (health OR healthcare) site:gov.uk, 1st 50 screened: 0 selected - age AND (barriers OR limits OR bias OR discrimination) AND (health OR healthcare) site:org.uk, 1st 100 screened: 3 selected - young AND (barriers OR limits OR bias OR discrimination) AND (health OR healthcare) site:nhs.uk, 1st 100 screened: 4 selected - young AND (barriers OR limits OR bias OR discrimination) AND (health OR healthcare) site:gov.uk, 1st 100 screened: 1 selected - young AND (barriers OR limits OR bias OR discrimination) AND (health OR healthcare) site:org.uk, 1st 100 screened: 1 selected - age AND (rationing OR "priority setting" OR "prioritisation") AND (health OR healthcare) site:nhs.uk, 1st 100 screened: 1 selected - age AND (rationing OR "priority setting" OR "prioritisation") AND (health OR healthcare) site:gov.uk, 1st 50 screened: 0 selected - age AND (rationing OR "priority setting" OR "prioritisation") AND (health OR healthcare) site:org.uk, 1st 100 screened: 4 selected - young AND (rationing OR "priority setting" OR "prioritisation") AND (health OR healthcare) site:nhs.uk, 1st 50 screened: 0 selected - young AND (rationing OR "priority setting" OR "prioritisation") AND (health OR healthcare) site:gov.uk, 1st 50 screened: 0 selected - young AND (rationing OR "priority setting" OR "prioritisation") AND (health OR healthcare) site:org.uk, 1st 50 screened: 0 selected Record the total number of selected results for web-based searches (21).
